content.thinking:
The user wants me to monitor a Spark cluster and manually scale workers based on traffic phases. Let me start by checking the current state of the cluster and then monitor the traffic generator logs to know when to scale.

Let me begin by checking the current state of the pods and deployments.

content.text: Good — cluster is running with 5 workers (Phase 1 baseline). Now let me check the traffic generator to see what phase we're in.

input.description: Check traffic generator logs for current phase

content.content:
PHASE 1: baseline load (5 workers sufficient) - 60s
LAG=2000 LATENCY=50ms

tool_use_result.stdout:
PHASE 1: baseline load (5 workers sufficient) - 60s
LAG=2000 LATENCY=50ms

content.text: Phase 1 is active. I'll poll the traffic generator logs, scaling at Phase 2 and 3 transitions, then scale back at Phase 4.

input.command:
# Poll until we see PHASE 2, then scale to 10
while true; do
  LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=5 2>&1)
  echo "[$(date +%T)] $LOGS"
  if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 2"; then
    echo ">>> PHASE 2 detected — scaling to 10 workers"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=10
    echo ">>> Scaled to 10"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Poll traffic generator until Phase 2 detected, then scale to 10 workers

input.command:
# Poll until we see PHASE 3, then scale to 20
while true; do
  LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=5 2>&1)
  echo "[$(date +%T)] $LOGS"
  if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 3"; then
    echo ">>> PHASE 3 detected — scaling to 20 workers"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=20
    echo ">>> Scaled to 20"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done

input.command:
# Poll until we see PHASE 4, then scale back to 5
while true; do
  LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=5 2>&1)
  echo "[$(date +%T)] $LOGS"
  if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 4"; then
    echo ">>> PHASE 4 detected — scaling back to 5 workers"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=5
    echo ">>> Scaled to 5"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Poll traffic generator until Phase 4 cooldown detected, then scale back to 5 workers
